field_decisions.value: Luxembourg linguistic leave is capped at 200 hours in two stages, and eligible Luxembourgish courses must come from recognised or approved provider categories.
field_decisions.reason: This is the cleanest official funding/leave route for the category.

field_decisions.value: INLL is the national certification body for Luxembourgish and the official Sproochentest provider, but the FAQ says the article-28 nationality courses are organised by the Adult Education Service or its contracted providers.
field_decisions.reason: This is the crucial separation between official proof routes and ordinary courses.

value: INLL free self-learning route for workplace Luxembourgish
field_decisions.reason: The public/private landscape is already visible without inventing demand or leadership claims.

value: A provider-issued CEFR-style certificate should not be marketed as automatically sufficient for official needs.
The narrow safe fact is that INLL does not offer the specific nationality-law article-28 courses; broader certificate-validity wording remains unsafe.
Only a free public self-learning route is evidenced for INLL; overcrowding and genericity are not.
No primary evidence in the rerun proves rising Luxembourgish demand for this exact product.
field_decisions.reason: These are the remaining fail-closed risks after the rerun.

field_decisions.value: Luxembourg employees and employers needing workplace Luxembourgish while navigating official leave and recognition constraints.
field_decisions.omission_reason: Useful synthesis, but still partly inferential for the exact product.
The rerun does not support treating a product certificate as automatically valid for official needs.
Only the existence of a free public route is evidenced.
No primary demand-growth metric survived the rerun.
